location.office_time_required: occasionally required to work out of assigned Affirm office (proximal roles only; this role appears fully remote)

level_signals: Base pay range $164K–$245K depending on location
Pay Grade M / Equity Grade 8 signals senior individual contributor or manager level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in product marketing, go-to-market strategy, enablement, consulting, or a related role, ideally in FinTech, payments, SaaS, or another high-growth environment.
Proven success translating product strategy into field-ready go-to-market plans that improved launch adoption, execution quality, or commercial outcomes.
Strong experience working cross-functionally with Product, Product Marketing, Sales leadership, Analytics, Legal, Pricing, and Enablement.
Ability to connect high-level product strategy to practical frontline execution across sales, partnerships, and customer-facing teams.
Demonstrated strength in asking sharp questions, driving clarity, and influencing decisions in ambiguous or rapidly evolving environments.
Strong strategic thinking and operating rigor, with experience leading complex launches, readiness efforts, or go-to-market motions end to end.
Experience building feedback loops between frontline teams and Product or Marketing to improve positioning, packaging, and execution.
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to use data and field insight to shape recommendations and measure success.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders across functions and seniority levels.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with merchant, payments, or fintech ecosystems is strongly preferred.

scope.surface: GTM strategy and operations across Revenue, Product, Pricing, Risk, and Legal teams; focus on product launches and merchant partnerships
scope.business_model: B2B2C — BNPL platform connecting merchants and consumers
core_responsibilities: Support product go-to-market execution for Revenue teams by helping translate product strategy into actionable plans
Partner with Product, Product Marketing, Legal, Pricing, and Revenue teams to support alignment on go-to-market execution
Support field and launch readiness efforts, ensuring materials and processes are aligned with go-to-market plans
Assist in translating product and marketing inputs into go-to-market plans for Revenue teams
Gather and share field feedback with Product and Marketing teams to help improve positioning and execution
Partner with GTM Enablement to ensure launch strategy is translated into effective readiness plans, field communications, and execution support
Monitor for gaps and risks and escalate issues to stakeholders to support timely resolution
Contribute input to prioritization discussions based on field insights and data
Analyze data and provide insights to support go-to-market decisions
Support cross-functional coordination to ensure alignment during product launches

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade 8; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan); equity rewards mentioned as part of total compensation

green_flags: Compensation ranges disclosed and competitive ($164K–$245K base depending on state)
Remote-first company with broad US remote eligibility
Clear description of cross-functional scope and responsibilities
Pay Grade M / Equity Grade 8 provides transparency on internal leveling
Equity and comprehensive benefits package explicitly described
Fair chance hiring policy stated for LA and SF applicants
red_flags: Role responsibilities are described with hedging language ('support', 'assist', 'contribute') which may indicate a more junior execution role despite 8+ years requirement
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-US-citizen candidates
Posting date not available; recency of opportunity unknown

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement, pay grade M, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; defaulted to false as no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd lower bound uses 'all other U.S. states' range; upper bound uses CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT range
ambiguities: The JD states '#LI-Remote' and 'remote-first company' suggesting fully remote eligibility, but also mentions 'proximal roles' may need occasional office time. It is unclear whether this specific role is proximal or fully remote.

The years requirement says 'ideally in FinTech, payments, SaaS' which could be read as hard or soft; classified as hard since it is in the main 'What We Look For' unconditional bullets, but the word 'ideally' introduces softness. Flagged here.
Compensation range spans two tiers ($164K–$224K for most states; $185K–$245K for CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT). The base_range_usd captures the full span across both tiers.
